text:
Arpit Kumble
AI/ML Engineer | Data Engineer | LLM Evaluation & Synthetic Data Pipelines
+91 7304377956 | arpitkumble@gmail.com  |  Linkedin  | Github

I am an early-career AI/ML and Data Engineering professional with hands-on experience in LLM evaluation, synthetic data pipelines, large-scale data processing, and foundation model development. I contributed to BharatGen at IIT Bombay, supporting large-scale AI initiatives involving 10M+ PDFs, 50TB+ curated fine-tuning datasets, evaluation of 30+ LLMs, and development of a 20K+ cybersecurity benchmark. I have a strong interest in building reliable data and AI systems at the intersection of data engineering, machine learning, and Generative AI, with a focus on learning quickly, solving practical problems, and contributing within high-performing technical teams.

BharatGen, IIT Bombay | Jan–May 2026
An IIT Bombay-led initiative advancing India’s multilingual LLM capabilities for Indian languages and generative AI use cases.
AI/ML Engineer
I contributed to building and optimizing large-scale ETL and data processing pipelines in Python, applying multiprocessing, Asyncio, and Aiohttp for distributed web crawling alongside AWS (S3, Lambda) to process over 10M+ PDFs for a multilingual foundation model initiative. This effort deepened my understanding of production-scale data infrastructure while also helping reduce pipeline execution time by 92%.
I ran systematic custom -based evaluations across 30+ LLMs using task-specific benchmarks, learning firsthand how model selection decisions are made at production scale; as part of this work, I independently designed and built a 20K+ cybersecurity benchmark dataset to assess and rank models across domain-specific tasks, my first end-to-end ownership of an evaluation framework.
I worked on high-performance deduplication  and data quality filtering pipelines processing 500M+ dataset rows, and contributed to synthetic data generation frameworks that curated 50TB+ of fine-tuning datasets for foundation model development gaining practical exposure to the full data curation lifecycle that underpins large-scale AI training.
PV Doctor Pte Ltd, Singapore | Oct–Dec 2025 
AI-powered Asset Performance Management company for solar PV systems, using real-time monitoring and predictive diagnostics to improve maintenance decisions and prevent energy yield losses.
Data Engineer | Asset Performance Analytics
I supported the migration, validation, and data consistency checks across 800+ solar monitoring sites for multiple clients, ensuring reliable site integrations, monitoring accuracy, and operational continuity across energy platforms.
I refactored Python automation scripts into scalable database-driven workflows using Azure SQL database, improving data retrieval efficiency, pipeline reliability, and observability through structured logging and exception handling.
I designed analytics dashboards and automated reporting pipelines for monitoring energy yield, system performance, and operational insights, additionally I integrated data pipelines across clients including Huawei, Sungrow, and SolarEdge, enabling unified performance monitoring across multiple client portfolios.
Independent AI Research & Project Development | Apr–Sep 2025 
LLM Fine-Tuning & Data Deduplication Pipeline
Developed an end-to-end supervised fine-tuning pipeline using PySpark, MinHashLSH, Jaccard Similarity, and large-scale data processing to deduplicate, clean, and preprocess 50M+ Parquet records for transformer model training on curated instruction datasets.

AWS, Mumbai | Jul–Nov 2022
Cloud Data Engineer
Developed cloud-native ETL and infrastructure workflows using AWS EC2, S3, Lambda, RDS, and EKS, supporting containerized deployments, monitoring systems, and scalable data engineering architectures.

Project CourseCrafterAI  | Jul 2024– Jan 2025
Developed a multi-tool Generative AI application using GPT-4, prompt engineering, Python, APIs, structured output generation, and tool/function calling to support six agentic workflows for curriculum ideation and automated instructional content generation.

Certificate Performance Analytics Platform  | Dec 2023– May 2024
Developed a scalable certificate performance analytics platform using MySQL, ETL pipelines, real-time data processing, and analytics dashboards to automate certificate ingestion, leaderboard updates, and role-based performance reporting
SKILLS TECHNOLOGY SKILLS
LLM & GenAI :	LangChain, LanGraph, OpenAI API, HuggingFace Transformers, LoRA, RAGAS, Prompt

[Repo: Arxiv Scrapping] [overview]
**ArXiv Complete PDF Downloader — Distributed, Fault-Tolerant, Resume-Safe
This project is a high-performance, distributed ArXiv PDF scraper designed for large-scale archival of all papers on ArXiv from 1991 to today.
It supports:

[Repo: Arxiv Scrapping] [overview]
1. Distributed scraping across multiple machines

[Repo: Arxiv Scrapping] [overview]
2. Resume capability — never loses progress

[Repo: Arxiv Scrapping] [overview]
8. Duplicate detection using a BloomFilter

[Repo: Arxiv Scrapping] [overview]
11. Asynchronous downloads (aiohttp + asyncio)

[Repo: Arxiv Scrapping] [overview]
Used properly, this system can download millions of PDFs safely.

[Repo: Arxiv Scrapping] [overview]
Split year ranges across machines so no machine downloads the same month twice.

[Repo: Arxiv Scrapping] [overview]
If the downloader stops, it restarts from where it left.

[Repo: Arxiv Scrapping] [overview]
Each month (e.g. 20150701_20150731) is marked completed after downloading papers.

[Repo: Arxiv Scrapping] [overview]
Every downloaded paper is stored in metadata_all.json.

[Repo: Arxiv Scrapping] [overview]
Using BloomFilter + memory checks to avoid reprocessing paper IDs.

[Repo: Arxiv Scrapping] [overview]
ArXiv categories changed over the years — your script automatically picks correct ones.

[Repo: Arxiv Scrapping] [overview]
PDFs are downloaded with retries and fallback HTTP download.

[Repo: Arxiv Scrapping] [overview]
aiohttp
asyncio.Semaphore
concurrent tasks
for efficient parallel downloading**.

[Repo: Minhash Lsh Dedup] [Minhash-LSH-Jaccard-Based-deduplication-on-parquet-datasets-pipeline]
High-performance near-duplicate detection for large Parquet datasets using MinHash signatures and Locality-Sensitive Hashing (LSH). Designed for 50M+ row datasets on multi-core HPC clusters.

---

[Repo: Minhash Lsh Dedup] [How It Works]
The pipeline runs in 5 sequential phases:



**Text input:** By default, `Question` and `Answer` columns are concatenated with `[SEP]` before hashing. Use `--question-only` to hash only the `Question` column.

**Duplicate policy:** For each confirmed near-duplicate pair `(a, b)` where `a < b`, row `b` is dropped. The first occurrence of each near-duplicate group is always kept.

---

[Repo: Minhash Lsh Dedup] [Usage]
### Basic



### Directory (processes all `.parquet` files recursively)



### Common options



---

[Repo: Minhash Lsh Dedup] [Arguments]
| Argument | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--input` | *(required)* | Input `.parquet` file or directory |
| `--output` | *(required)* | Output file or directory |
| `--workers` | `100` | Number of parallel worker processes |
| `--threshold` | `0.7` | Jaccard similarity threshold (0–1). Lower = more aggressive dedup |
| `--num-perm` | `128` | Number of MinHash permutations. Higher = more accurate, more RAM |
| `--ngram` | `3` | Character n-gram size for shingling |
| `--chunk` | `500000` | Rows per batch for reading/writing |
| `--no-verify` | `False` | Skip Jaccard verification — faster but less precise |
| `--question-only` | `False` | Hash only `Question` column instead of `Question + Answer` |
| `--skip-existing` | `False` | Skip output files that already exist |
| `--max-ram-per-worker` | `4.0` | GB RAM budget per Jaccard worker |
| `--tasks-per-worker` | `4` | Target Jaccard tasks per worker for load balancing |
| `--min-task-size` | `100000` | Minimum pairs per Jaccard task |
| `--max-task-size` | `10000000` | Maximum pairs per Jaccard task |

[Repo: Minhash Lsh Dedup] [Threshold Guide]
| `--threshold` | Meaning | Use when |
|---|---|---|
| `0.95+` | Near-exact duplicates only | Text with minor edits (whitespace, punctuation) |
| `0.8–0.95` | High similarity | Paraphrased or lightly reworded content |
| `0.7` *(default)* | Moderate similarity | Standard deduplication |
| `0.5–0.7` | Loose similarity | Aggressive dedup, topic-level clustering |

---

[Repo: Minhash Lsh Dedup] [Architecture Details]
Each row is converted to a set of character n-gram shingles, then hashed into a `num_perm`-dimensional MinHash signature. Work is split across `--workers` processes using `multiprocessing.Pool`.

[Repo: Minhash Lsh Dedup] [Architecture Details]
Signatures are written to a **memory-mapped NumPy file** (`signatures.npy`) rather than held in RAM. This allows datasets larger than available RAM to be processed.

[Repo: Minhash Lsh Dedup] [Architecture Details]
All signatures are streamed through a `MinHashLSH` index. For each row, the index is queried for near-neighbours before the row itself is inserted. This yields a set of **candidate pairs** — rows that are likely near-duplicates but not yet confirmed.

[Repo: Minhash Lsh Dedup] [Architecture Details]
LSH is approximate by design — it trades a small number of false negatives for O(n) query time instead of O(n²).

[Repo: Minhash Lsh Dedup] [Architecture Details]
### Phase 3 — Jaccard Verification (parallel)

[Repo: Minhash Lsh Dedup] [Architecture Details]
Candidate pairs are verified by computing the exact Jaccard similarity from their MinHash signatures:

[Repo: Minhash Lsh Dedup] [Architecture Details]
Pairs below `--threshold` are rejected. This step reads signatures from the memory-mapped file directly in each worker — **no signature data is pickled or sent over IPC**.

[Repo: Minhash Lsh Dedup] [Architecture Details]
Task size is computed dynamically based on `--max-ram-per-worker` and `--tasks-per-worker` to avoid OOM errors.

[Repo: Minhash Lsh Dedup] [Architecture Details]
For all confirmed pairs `(a, b)` with `a < b`, row `b` is added to a `duplicates` set. The keep set is `all_rows - duplicates`. This is O(n_pairs) with no graph traversal.

[Repo: Minhash Lsh Dedup] [Architecture Details]
The original file is re-read in chunks. Each chunk is filtered to keep only rows in the keep set, then written to the output Parquet file with Snappy compression.

[Repo: Minhash Lsh Dedup] [Memory Usage]
| Component | Size |
|---|---|
| Signatures memmap | `total_rows × num_perm × 8 bytes` |
| Candidate pairs | `n_pairs × 8 bytes` (int32 × 2) |
| Jaccard RAM/worker | `task_size × num_perm × 16 bytes` |

**Example:** 10M rows, `num_perm=128` → signatures memmap ≈ **10 GB**. Use a fast local disk or tmpfs for the temp directory if possible.

---

[Repo: Minhash Lsh Dedup] [Output]
- Output Parquet files are named `{stem}_deduped.parquet`
- Schema is preserved exactly from the input
- Compressed with Snappy
- Row order is preserved (first occurrence of each group is kept)

---

[Repo: Minhash Lsh Dedup] [Tips]
**Speed up Phase 1** — increase `--workers` and `--chunk`. MinHash is CPU-bound and scales linearly with cores.

**Speed up Phase 2** — LSH is single-threaded and the bottleneck for very large datasets (10M+ rows). Lower `--num-perm` reduces LSH insert/query time at the cost of accuracy.

**Reduce memory** — lower `--num-perm` (try `64`) or `--max-ram-per-worker`.

**Stricter dedup** — raise `--threshold` toward `0.9`. Looser dedup — lower toward `0.5`.

**Large directories** — use `--skip-existing` to resume interrupted runs without reprocessing completed files.

[Repo: Ats Resume Analyser] [ATS RAG Analyzer - Intelligent Resume Screening System]
A modern, full-stack **Applicant Tracking System (ATS)** powered by **Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)** that intelligently analyzes resumes against job descriptions using AI-driven insights.

![Stack](https://img.shields.io/badge/Stack-React%20%7C%20TypeScript%20%7C%20TanStack%20%7C%20FastAPI%20%7C%20LangChain-blue)
![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green)

---

metadata.heading: ATS RAG Analyzer - Intelligent Resume Screening System

text:
[Repo: Ats Resume Analyser] [Project Overview]
**ATS RAG Analyzer** is an intelligent resume screening platform that uses advanced RAG technology to:

- Upload resumes and job descriptions in **PDF/DOCX** formats
- Get **AI-powered match analysis** with percentage scores
- Run **session-isolated analysis** (each session has its own vector database)
- Ask follow-up questions via **conversational chat**
- Maintain complete **session history** with rename, duplicate, and delete features

The system leverages **LangChain + ChromaDB + OpenAI** to provide semantic understanding and context-aware recommendations.

---

[Repo: Ats Resume Analyser] [Key Features]
###  Resume Matching
- **Intelligent Analysis**: AI-driven matching between resume and job description
- **Match Score**: Percentage-based compatibility score (0-100%)
- **Detailed Verdict**: Comprehensive analysis with recommendations

[Repo: Ats Resume Analyser] [Key Features]
###  Session Management
- **Isolated Storage**: Each session gets its own ChromaDB vector database
- **Session Persistence**: Store, view, rename, duplicate, and delete sessions
- **No Cross-Contamination**: Complete data isolation between sessions

[Repo: Ats Resume Analyser] [Key Features]
###  Interactive Chat
- **Follow-up Questions**: Ask clarification questions about the analysis
- **Conversation History**: Maintain context across multiple messages
- **RAG-Powered Responses**: Answers backed by actual resume/JD content

[Repo: Ats Resume Analyser] [Key Features]
###  Analysis Dashboard
- **Real-time Metrics**: Match score, job role detection
- **Key Insights**: Strengths, gaps, and recommendations
- **Visual Feedback**: Progress indicators and status badges

[Repo: Ats Resume Analyser] [Key Features]
###  Developer-Friendly
- **Type-Safe**: Full TypeScript implementation
- **RESTful API**: Clean, well-documented endpoints
- **Environment Config**: Flexible deployment configuration

[Repo: Ats Resume Analyser] [Tech Stack]
### Infrastructure & DevOps
- **Cloudflare Workers**: Edge deployment (via Wrangler)

[Repo: Ats Resume Analyser] [Quick Start]
### Prerequisites
- **Node.js** 18+ (or **Bun** runtime)
- **Python** 3.10+
- **pip** package manager
- **OpenAI API Key** (OpenRouter or Direct)

### 1. Clone & Install

```bash

[Repo: Ats Resume Analyser] [Clone repository]
git clone https://github.com/Arpit2811/Rag_based_ATS_Analyser.git
cd my_rag/Rag_based_ats_analyser

[Repo: Ats Resume Analyser] [Setup Python backend]
python -m venv rag_venv
rag_venv\Scripts\activate  # Windows

[Repo: Ats Resume Analyser] [or: source rag_venv/bin/activate  # macOS/Linux]
pip install fastapi uvicorn python-multipart python-dotenv \
  langchain langchain-community langchain-chroma langchain-openai \
  langchain-text-splitters pypdf docx2txt
env

[Repo: Ats Resume Analyser] [OpenAI Configuration]
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-your-api-key-here
OPENAI_BASE_URL=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1  # or https://api.openai.com/v1

[Repo: Ats Resume Analyser] [CORS Configuration (comma-separated)]
CORS_ORIGINS=http://localhost:5173,https://your-deployed-app.com
bash
cd python-backend
uvicorn server:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --reload
bash
npm run dev

[Repo: Ats Resume Analyser] [or: bun run dev]
```

App runs at: `http://localhost:5173`

### Data Flow



### Session Isolation

Each session gets:
- Dedicated ChromaDB collection: `chroma_db/{sessionId}/`
- Isolated upload directory: `uploads/{sessionId}/`
- No data leakage between sessions
- Session-specific chat history

```bash

[Repo: Ats Resume Analyser] [Get analysis]
analysis = requests.post(
    "http://localhost:8000/sessions/session-123/analyze"
).json()

print(f"Match Score: {analysis['matchScore']}%")
print(f"Verdict: {analysis['verdict']}")
```

[Repo: Sft Model Training] [overview]
This repository provides a pipeline for Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) of large language models using Hugging Face Transformers, Datasets, and PyTorch. It supports loading datasets from Parquet or JSON formats, processing conversational data, tokenizing inputs, and training models efficiently on GPU-enabled systems.

[Repo: Sft Model Training] [overview]
The project is designed for scalability and can be adapted for large datasets and multi-GPU environments.

[Repo: Sft Model Training] [overview]
Features
Support for chat-based datasets with role and content structure
Efficient dataset loading from Parquet and Hugging Face datasets
Batch tokenization pipeline
Configurable training parameters
Logging for monitoring training and debugging
Compatible with large-scale training setups
Project Structure
SFT_MODEL_TRAINING/

[Repo: Sft Model Training] [overview]
├── data/                  # Input datasets
├── tokenizer/             # Saved tokenizer files
├── logs/                  # Log files

[Repo: Sft Model Training] [overview]
├── train.py               # Main training script
├── preprocess.py          # Data preprocessing logic
├── utils.py               # Utility functions
├── config.py              # Configuration settings

[Repo: Sft Model Training] [overview]
├── requirements.txt
└── README.md
Installation
Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/Arpit2811/SFT_MODEL_TRAINING.git
cd SFT_MODEL_TRAINING
Create a virtual environment
python -m venv env
source env/bin/activate

[Repo: Sft Model Training] [overview]
env\Scripts\activate
Install dependencies
pip install -r requirements.txt
Dataset Format

[Repo: Sft Model Training] [overview]
The pipeline expects conversational data in the following format:

[Repo: Sft Model Training] [overview]
If using Parquet files, the data should typically be stored under a column such as messages containing the same structure.

[Repo: Sft Model Training] [overview]
The preprocessing pipeline performs the following steps:

[Repo: Sft Model Training] [overview]
Loads dataset from Parquet or JSON files
Extracts role and content fields
Formats conversations into a training-ready structure
Applies tokenization using a Hugging Face tokenizer
Training

[Repo: Sft Model Training] [overview]
Training parameters can be configured in the configuration file or directly in the script. Typical parameters include:

[Repo: Sft Model Training] [overview]
BATCH_SIZE = 8
LEARNING_RATE = 2e-5
EPOCHS = 3
MAX_LENGTH = 2048
Logging

[Repo: Sft Model Training] [overview]
Logs are stored in the logs/ directory. These include:

[Repo: Sft Model Training] [overview]
Dataset processing progress
Training status
Error and debug information
Output

[Repo: Sft Model Training] [overview]
After training, the following outputs are generated:

[Repo: Sft Model Training] [overview]
Model checkpoints in the output directory
Tokenizer files in the tokenizer directory
Performance Considerations
Use mixed precision (fp16 or bfloat16) for faster training
Adjust batch size based on available GPU memory
Use gradient accumulation for large models
Prefer Parquet format for handling large datasets
Future Enhancements
Integration with parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods such as LoRA
Support for distributed training frameworks such as DeepSpeed
Evaluation and benchmarking pipeline
Inference utilities
License

[Repo: Zlib Processing] [Corpus Builder (Parallel Mode)]
A high‑performance corpus extraction pipeline designed to process large
collections of document metadata and extract clean textual data.

This tool supports multiple document formats and uses **parallel
multiprocessing** for faster processing. It is designed for
**large‑scale text corpus building**, dataset preparation, and NLP
pipelines.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Repo: Zlib Processing] [Features]
-   Parallel processing using Python multiprocessing
-   Native extraction for:
    -   EPUB
    -   FB2
    -   DOCX
    -   TXT
    -   HTML
-   Complex extraction for:
    -   MOBI
    -   AZW / AZW3
    -   PRC / PDB
-   Automatic fallback to **Apache Tika** for unsupported formats
-   Removes FB2 binary image data to prevent garbage text
-   Encoding issue detection
-   Automatic word and page estimation
-   Robust logging system
-   Resilient file resolution logic

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Repo: Zlib Processing] [Supported Formats]
Format              Extraction Method
  ------------------- ----------------------
  TXT                 Native
  HTML / HTM          Native
  EPUB                Native
  FB2                 Native
  DOCX                Native
  MOBI / AZW / AZW3   Complex Extraction
  PRC / PDB           Complex Extraction
  XML / OPF           Markup Parsing
  Others              Apache Tika Fallback

Ignored formats include:

    jpg jpeg png exe lnk db iso rar zip djvu pdf

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Repo: Zlib Processing] [Architecture]
Pipeline Flow

    metadata.jsonl
          │
          ▼
    Parallel Workers
          │
          ▼
    File Resolver
          │
          ▼
    Extractor Dispatcher
          │
     ┌────┼───────────────┐
     │    │               │
    Native Extractors   MOBI Handler
     │                  │
     ▼                  ▼
    Clean Text Output
          │
          ▼
    JSONL Corpus File

------------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Repo: Zlib Processing] [Logging]
Logs are written to the file specified with:

    --log extraction.log

Logs include:

-   file processing
-   encoding errors
-   missing files
-   extraction failures
-   periodic progress updates

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Repo: Zlib Processing] [Performance]
Parallel mode allows scaling to very large datasets.

Typical performance:

  Workers   Files / Minute
  --------- ----------------
  4         \~200
  8         \~450
  16        \~900

Actual performance depends on disk speed, file size, and document type.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Repo: Zlib Processing] [Dependencies]
Main libraries used:

-   beautifulsoup4
-   lxml
-   mobi
-   tika
-   multiprocessing (Python standard library)

Full list available in `requirements.txt`.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Repo: Zlib Processing] [Use Cases]
-   NLP corpus building
-   Large dataset preparation for LLM training
-   Digital library ingestion pipelines
-   Research dataset generation
-   Document text extraction pipelines
